title: Separate Unused parameters from Unused signals
body:
I recently updated my verilator and noticed this change (https://github.com/verilator/verilator/issues/2627). I know it has been discussed in here (https://github.com/verilator/verilator/issues/2798), but I want to give a second opinion.

I like that in verilator, you can quickly check for errors in a file by running `verilator --lint-only -Wall "filename1.sv"`. I find it the fastest way to fix mistakes in code without having to lint the code. Unused signal id usually stuff not properly wired, while unused parameter is rarely a mistake. For example, you set up the parameters for a standardized interface, but you are only using a few parameters. Now you will get a warning for each unused parameter. 

I think it would be great to be able to do something like this `verilator --lint-only -Wall -Wno-unused-parameter "filename1.sv"` to fix the signals without having to look at the parameters.
